{{Quote|Ajunta Pall beheaded their ruler, Hakagram Graush, and claimed the throne as the blood heir to the ancestral King Adas—and we became his Shadow Hands.|Sorzus Syn|<ref name="BoS" />}}
'''Shadow Hand''' was the title given to the second-in-command of the [[Sith King]].<ref name="END" />

Later, [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] orders would use it as both a title for the second-in-command of the [[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]] and as a term equivalent to a [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]].<ref name="END" />

The [[Adas's Shadow Hand|Shadow Hand]] of [[King/Legends|King]] [[Adas]] would inherit the [[Death/Legends|fallen]] king's [[Holocron of King Adas|holocron]]; while another [[Hakagram Graush's Shadow Hand|Shadow Hand]]<ref name="END" /> of King [[Hakagram Graush]],<ref name="BoS" /> many thousands of years later, betrayed his master to the newly arrived [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Exiles|Exile]]<ref name="END" /> [[Ajunta Pall]], thus paving the way for Pall to become the first [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]. After Hakagram Graush's death, Pall appointed his fellow Dark Jedi as his new Shadow Hands.<ref name="BoS" />

During the [[Krath Holy Crusade]], the [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] of long-dead Dark Lord of the Sith [[Marka Ragnos]] branded [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] with the [[Mark of Shadow Hand|mark of a Shadow Hand]],<ref name="KOTORCG" /> whom Ragnos said would become [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]]'s first and foremost apprentice.<ref name="DLOTS6">[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 6|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 6]]</ref> Qel-Droma then served as Kun's Shadow Hand throughout the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]].<ref name="END" /> During the [[Jedi Civil War]], after disposing of his [[Revan/Legends|master]], [[Darth Malak]] declared his apprentice [[Darth Bandon]] to be his Shadow Hand. A few years later, during the [[First Jedi Purge]], [[Visas Marr]] served as [[Darth Nihilus]]'s Shadow Hand after Nihilus retrieved her from the surface of her [[Force drain/Legends|Force drained]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], [[Katarr/Legends|Katarr]].<ref name="KOTORCG" /> The concept continued with later Sith. [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] [[Sith training|trained]] his own disciples, among them [[Lumiya]] and [[Flint]], in the hopes of pitting them against one another to find a champion worthy of being the Dark Lord's own Shadow Hand. Prior to this, [[Darth Cognus]], who, like Vader, was a Sith of the [[Order of the Sith Lords|lineage]] of [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]], trained her apprentice [[Darth Millennial]] as a Shadow Hand.<ref name="END" />

The reborn [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] used the name for his [[Operation Shadow Hand|campaign to retake the galaxy]].<ref name="de2 1">[[Dark Empire II 1|''Dark Empire II'' 1]] {{C|opening crawl}}</ref>
